author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XIII. 24-30.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Another parable put He forth unto them, saying, The Kingdom of Heaven is likened unto a man which sowed good seed in his field.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But while men slept, his enemy came and sowed tares among the wheat, and went his way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But when the blade was sprung up, and brought forth fruit, then appeared the tares.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So the servants of the householder came and said unto him, Sir, didst thou not sow good seed in thy field? whence then hath it tares?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He said unto them, An enemy hath done this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The servants said unto him, Wilt thou then that we go and gather them up?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But he said, Nay, lest while ye gather up the tares, ye root up also the wheat with them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let both therefore grow together until the harvest.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What is the difference between this, and the parable before it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: There He speaks of them that have not at all holden with Him, but have started aside, and have thrown away the seed; but here He means the societies of the heretics.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in order that not even this might disturb His disciples, He foretells it also, after having taught them why He speaks in parables.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The former parable then means their not receiving Him; this, their receiving corrupters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed this also is a part of the devil’s craft, by the side of the truth always to bring in error, painting thereon many resemblances, so as easily to cheat the deceivable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He calls it not any other seed, but tares; which in appearance are somewhat like wheat.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then He mentions also the manner of his device.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “while men slept,” saith He.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is no small danger, which He hereby suspends over our rulers, to whom especially is entrusted the keeping of the field; and not the rulers only, but the subjects too.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He signifies also that the error comes after the truth, which the actual event testifies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so after the prophets, were the false prophets; and after the apostles, the false apostles; and after Christ, Antichrist.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For unless the devil see what to imitate, or against whom to plot, he neither attempts, nor knows how.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now then also, having seen that “one brought forth a hundred, another sixty, another thirty,” he proceeds after that another way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, not having been able to carry away what had taken root, nor to choke, nor to scorch it up, he conspires against it by another craft, privily casting in his own inventions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what difference is there, one may say, between them that sleep, and them that resemble the wayside?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That in the latter case he immediately caught it away; yea, he suffered it not even to take root; but here more of his craft was needed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And these things Christ saith, instructing us to be always wakeful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, saith He, though thou quite escape those harms, there is yet another harm.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as in those instances “the wayside,” and “the rock,” and “the thorns,” so here again sleep occasions our ruin; so that there is need of continual watchfulness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore He also said, “He that endureth to the end, the same shall be saved.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Something like this took place even at the beginning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Many of the prelates, I mean, bringing into the churches wicked men, disguised heresiarchs, gave great facility to the laying that kind of snare.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the devil needs not even to take any trouble, when he hath once planted them among us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how is it possible not to sleep? one may say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Indeed, as to natural sleep, it is not possible; but as to that of our moral faculty, it is possible.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore Paul also said, “Watch ye, stand fast in the faith.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After this He points out the thing to be superfluous too, not hurtful only; in that, after the land hath been tilled, and there is no need of anything, then this enemy sows again; as the heretics also do, who for no other cause than vainglory inject their proper venom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not by this only, but by what follows likewise, He depicts exactly all their acting.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “When the blade was sprung up,” saith He, “and brought forth fruit, then appeared the tares also;” which kind of thing these men also do.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For at the beginning they disguise themselves; but when they have gained much confidence, and some one imparts to them the teaching of the word, then they pour out their poison.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But wherefore doth He bring in the servants, telling what hath been done?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That He may pronounce it wrong to slay them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He calls him “an enemy,” because of his harm done to men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For although the despite is against us, in its origin it sprang from his enmity, not to us, but to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence it is manifest, that God loves us more than we love ourselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And see from another thing also, the malicious craft of the devil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he did not sow before this, because he had nothing to destroy, but when all had been fulfilled, that he might defeat the diligence of the Husbandman; in such enmity against Him did he constantly act.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And mark also the affection of the servants.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I mean, what haste they are in at once to root up the tares, even though they do it indiscreetly; which shows their anxiety for the crop, and that they are looking to one thing only, not to the punishment of that enemy, but to the preservation of the seed sown.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For of course this other is not the urgent consideration.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore how they may for the present extirpate the mischief, this is their object.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not even this do they seek absolutely, for they trust not themselves with it, but await the Master’s decision, saying, “Wilt Thou?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then doth the Master?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He forbids them, saying, “Lest haply ye root up the wheat with them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this He said, to hinder wars from arising, and blood and slaughter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is not right to put a heretic to death, since an implacable war would be brought into the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By these two reasons then He restrains them; one, that the wheat be not hurt; another, that punishment will surely overtake them, if incurably diseased.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore, if thou wouldest have them punished, yet without harm to the wheat, I bid thee wait for the proper season.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But what means, “Lest ye root up the wheat with them?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Either He means this, If ye are to take up arms, and to kill the heretics, many of the saints also must needs be overthrown with them; or that of the very tares it is likely that many may change and become wheat.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If therefore ye root them up beforehand, ye injure that which is to become wheat, slaying some, in whom there is yet room for change and improvement.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He doth not therefore forbid our checking heretics, and stopping their mouths, and taking away their freedom of speech, and breaking up their assemblies and confederacies, but our killing and slaying them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But mark thou His gentleness, how He not only gives sentence and forbids, but sets down reasons.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then, if the tares should remain until the end?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Then I will say to the reapers, Gather ye together first the tares, and bind them in bundles to burn them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He again reminds them of John’s words,introducing Him as judge; and He saith, So long as they stand by the wheat, we must spare them, for it is possible for them even to become wheat but when they have departed, having profited nothing, then of necessity the inexorable punishment will overtake them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For I will say to the reapers,” saith He, “Gather ye together first the tares.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, “first?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That these may not be alarmed, as though the wheat were carried off with them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And bind them in bundles to burn them, but gather the wheat into my barn.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Another parable put He forth unto them, saying, The Kingdom of Heaven is like to a grain of mustard seed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, since He had said, that of the crop three parts are lost, and but one saved, and in the very part again which is saved so great damage ensues; lest they should say, “And who, and how many will be the faithful?” this fear again He removes, by the parable of the mustard seed leading them on to belief, and signifying that in any case the gospelshall be spread abroad.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He brought forward the similitude of this herb, which has a very strong resemblance to the subject in hand; “Which indeed is the least,” He saith, “of all seeds, but when it is grown, it is the greatest among herbs, and becometh a tree, so that the birds of the air come and lodge in the branches thereof.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus He meant to set forth the most decisive sign of its greatness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Even so then shall it be with respect to the gospel too,” saith He.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, for His disciples were weakest of all, and least of all; but nevertheless, because of the great power that was in them, It hath been unfoldedin every part of the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After this He adds the leaven to this similitude, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “The Kingdom of Heaven is like unto leaven, which a woman took, and hid in three measures of meal, until the whole was leavened.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as this converts the large quantity of meal into its own quality,even so shall ye convert the whole world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And see His wisdom, in that He brings in things natural, implying that as the one cannot fail to take place, so neither the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For say not this to me: “What shall we be able to do, twelve men, throwing ourselves upon so vast a multitude?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, for this very thing most of all makes your might conspicuous, that ye mix with the multitude and are not put to flight.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As therefore the leaven then leavens the lump when it comes close to the meal, and not simply close, but so as to be actually mixed with it (for He said not, “put,” simply, but “hid”); so also ye, when ye cleave to your enemies, and are made one with them, then shall ye get the better of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And as the leaven, though it be buried, yet is not destroyed, but by little and little transmutes all into its own condition; of like sort will the event be here also, with respect to the gospel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Fear ye not then, because I said there would be much injurious dealing: for even so shall ye shine forth, and get the better of all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But by “three measures,” here, He meant many, for He is wont to take this number for a multitude.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And marvel not, if discoursing about the kingdom, He made mention of a little seed and of leaven; for He was discoursing with men inexperienced and ignorant, and such as needed to be led on by those means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For so simple were they, that even after all this, they required a good deal of explanation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Where now are the children of the Greeks?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let them learn Christ’s power, seeing the verity of His deeds, and on either ground let them adore Him, that He both foretold so great a thing, and fulfilled it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, for it is He that put the power into the leaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With this intent He mingled also with the multitude those who believe on Him, that we might impart unto the rest of our wisdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let no one therefore reprove us for being few.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For great is the power of the gospel, and that which hath been once leavened, becomes leaven again for what remains.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And as a spark, when it hath caught in timber, makes what hath been burnt up already increase the flame, and so proceeds to the rest; even so the gospel likewise.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He said not fire, but “leaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why might this be?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because in that case the whole effect is not of the fire, but partly of the timber too that is kindled, but in this the leaven doth the whole work by itself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now if twelve men leavened the whole world, imagine how great our baseness, in that when we being so many are not able to amend them that remain; we, who ought to be enough for ten thousand worlds, and to become leaven to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But they,” one may say, “were apostles.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what then?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Were they not partakers with thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Were they not brought up in cities?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Did they not enjoy the same benefits?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Did they not practise trades?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What, were they angels?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What, came they down from Heaven?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But they had signs,” it will be said.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It was not the signs that made them admirable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How long shall we use those miracles as cloaks for our own remissness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Behold the choir of the Saints, that they shone not by those miracles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, many who had actually cast out devils, because they wrought iniquity, instead of being admired, did even incur punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what can it be then, he will say, that showed them great?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Their contempt of wealth, their despising glory, their freedom from worldly things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since surely, had they wanted these qualities, and been slaves of their passions, though they had raised ten thousand dead, so far from doing any good, they would even have been accounted deceivers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus it is their life, so bright on all sides, which also draws down the grace of the Spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What manner of miracle did John work, that he fixed on himself the attentionof so many cities?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For as to the fact that he did no wondrous works, hear the evangelist, saying, “John did no miracle.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And whence did Elias become admirable?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Was it not from his boldness towards the king? from his zeal towards God? from his voluntary poverty? from his garment of sheep’s skin, and his cave, and his mountains?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For his miracles he did after all these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And as to Job, what manner of miracle did he work in sight of the devil, that he was amazed at him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: No miracle indeed, but a life that shone and displayed an endurance firmer than any adamant.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What manner of miracle did David, yet being young, that God should say, “I have found David the son of Jesse, a man after mine own heart?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob, what dead body did they raise? what leper did they cleanse?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Knowest thou not that the miracles, except we be sober, do even harm in many cases?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus many of the Corinthians were severed one from another; thus many of the Romans were carried away with pride; thus was Simon cast out.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus he, who at a certain time had a desire to follow Christ, was rejected, when he had been told, “The foxes have holes, and the birds of the air nests.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For each of these, one aiming at the wealth, another at the glory, which the miracles bring, fell away and perished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But care of practice, and love of virtue, so far from generating such a desire, doth even take it away when it exists.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Himself too, when He was making laws for His own disciples, what said He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Do miracles, that men may see you”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By no means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Let your light shine before men, that they may see your good works, and glorify your Father which is in Heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And to Peter again He said not, “If thou lovest me,” “do miracles,” but “feed my sheep.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And whereas He everywhere distinguishes him with James and John above all the rest, for what, I pray thee, did He distinguish them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For their miracles?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, all alike cleansed the lepers, and raised the dead; and to all alike He gave that authority.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence then had these the advantage?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: From the virtue in their soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how everywhere practice is required, and the proof by works?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For by their fruits,” saith He, “ye shall know them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what commends our own life?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Is it indeed a display of miracles, or the perfection of an excellent conversation?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Very evidently it is the second; but as to the miracles, they both have their origin from hence, and terminate herein.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For both he that shows forth an excellent life, draws to himself this gift, and he that receives the gift, receives it for this end, that he may amend other men’s lives.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since even Christ for this end wrought those miracles, that having made Himself thereby credible, and drawn men unto Him, He might bring virtue into our life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also He lays more stress of the two on this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He is not at all satisfied with the signs only, but He also threatens hell, and promises a kingdom, and lays down those startling laws, and all things He orders to this end, that He may make us equal to the angels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And why say I, that Christ doth all for this object?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, even thou, should one give thee thy choice, to raise dead men by His name, or to die for His name; which I pray thee, of the two wouldest thou rather accept?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Is it not quite plain, the latter? and yet the one is a miracle, the other but a work.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what, if one offered thee to make grass gold, or to be able to despise all wealth as grass, wouldest thou not rather accept this latter? and very reasonably.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For mankind would be attracted by this more than any way.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if they saw the grass changed into gold, they would covet themselves also to acquire that power, as Simon did, and the love of money would be increased in them; but if they saw us all contemning and neglecting gold, as though it were grass, they would long ago have been delivered from this disease.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou that our practice has more power to do good?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By practice I mean, not thy fasting, nor yet thy strewing sackcloth and ashes under thee, but if thou despise wealth, as it ought to be despised; if thou be kindly affectioned, if thou give thy bread to the hungry, if thou control anger, if thou cast out vainglory, if thou put away envy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So He Himself used to teach: for, “Learn of me,” saith He, “for I am meek and lowly in heart.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He did not say, “for I fasted,” although surely He might have spoken of the forty days, yet He saith not this; but, “I am meek and lowly in heart.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again, when sending them out, He said not, “Fast,” but, “Eat of all that is set before you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With regard to wealth, however, He required of them great strictness, saying, “Provide not gold, or silver, or brass, in your purses.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And all this I say, not to depreciate fasting, God forbid, but rather highly to commend it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But I grieve when other duties being neglected, ye think it enough for salvation, having but the last place in the choir of virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the greatest thing is charity, and moderation, and almsgiving; which hits a higher mark even than virginity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore, if thou desire to become equal to the apostles, there is nothing to hinder thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to have arrived at this virtue only suffices for thy not at all falling short of them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let no one therefore wait for miracles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though the evil spirit is grieved, when he is driven out of a body, yet much more so, when he sees a soul delivered from sin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed this is his great power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This power caused Christ to die, that He might put an end to it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, for this brought in death; by reason of this all things have been turned upside down.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If then thou remove this, thou hast cut out the nerves of the devil, thou hast “bruised his head,” thou hast put an end to all his might, thou hast scattered his host, thou hast exhibited a sign greater than all signs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The saying is not mine, but the blessed Paul’s.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when he had said, “Covet earnestly the best gifts, and yet show I unto you a more excellent way;”\he did not speak next of a sign, but of charity, the root of all our good things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If then we practise this, and all the self-denial that flows from it, we shall have no need of signs; even as on the other hand, if we do not practise it, we shall gain nothing by the signs.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Bearing in mind then all this, let us imitate those things whereby the apostles became great.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And whereby did they become great?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hear Peter, saying, “Behold we have forsaken all, and followed Thee; what shall we have therefore?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hear also Christ saying to them, “Ye shall sit upon twelve thrones,” and, “every one that hath forsaken houses, or brethren, or father, or mother, shall receive an hundredfold in this world, and shall inherit everlasting life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: From all worldly things, therefore, let us withdraw ourselves, and dedicate ourselves to Christ, that we may both be made equal to the apostles according to His declaration, and may enjoy eternal life; unto which may we all attain, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ to whom be glory and might forever and ever.
